subject: DevOps
title: Google Cloud's 2019 Networking Outage
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Google Cloud's 2019 Networking Outage.

hook.text: June 2, 2019. Google Cloud, YouTube, and Gmail went down. A config change cascaded globally.

buildup.text: A network config change meant for one region was applied to multiple regions by mistake.

discovery.text: Traffic was routed to too few servers. Those servers overloaded and dropped requests.

twist.text: The misconfiguration spread because the safety system that should have blocked it had a bug.

climax.text: YouTube, Snapchat, and Discord all went down. Google's outage rippled across the internet.

punchline.text: Safety systems need testing too. Guard the guards.

quiz.question: Why did the Google Cloud 2019 outage spread so widely?
options: The safety system that should have prevented spread had a bug
Google had only one data center
